title: umbilicus horizontalis subsp. gaditanus
intro: Umbilicus horizontalis subsp. gaditanus, also known as the Origanum gaditanum, is a plant species within the Lamiaceae family, native to the Canary Islands. It possesses medicinal properties due to its rich content of flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. Traditionally, it is used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, as well as in the form of essential oils and extracts, to support overall well-being and promote natural healing processes.
